behaviors.description: The primary translator produces a grammatically coherent translation that preserves the meaning of the source text, not just a word-for-word substitution.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is model judgment quality (fluency + adequacy), which a stub cannot represent (LLM-DEP=yes). Candidate tests: Agentic setup: run real NMT API on a bilingual evaluation set; assertion: outputs meet fluency/meaning rubric or semantic-similarity threshold; catches: model regressions that become literal or ungrammatical. Adjacent Integration setup: stub API with canned good outputs; assertion: service returns them; catches wiring only, misses true translation-quality failures. Rejected Workflow: behavior specifies a single translation decision, not multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real translation model on a fixed multilingual benchmark (including idioms and syntax-heavy sentences) and score adequacy/fluency against references; fail if coherence/meaning preservation drops below threshold.

behaviors.description: When the translation API returns a 503 error, the service transparently falls back to the dictionary-based translator without returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Failure is module handoff/orchestration: API error handling -> fallback path -> caller response (MOCK-MISS at module boundary). REAL-INFRA: real network not required; an in-process fake client returning 503 reproduces this behavior-specific failure. Candidate tests: Integration setup: wire service + fake API client(503) + real fallback component; assertion: non-error response with fallback translation; catches broken fallback routing. Adjacent System setup: real HTTP server returning 503; assertion same; catches transport issues, but behavior text focuses on 503 handling logic, so System is not minimum.
behaviors.test_description: Run service with a test double for primary API that deterministically returns HTTP 503, plus dictionary translator wired normally; verify caller receives successful translated output and no propagated error.

behaviors.description: The dictionary-based fallback returns the correct pre-stored translation for phrases that exist in the SQLite database.
behaviors.justification: Behavior depends on SQL/schema/query + translator module interaction with local SQLite (multiple modules + local DB). Candidate tests: Integration setup: seed SQLite with phrase->translation rows and call fallback translator; assertion: exact stored translation returned; catches query mismatch/collation/schema bugs. Adjacent Unit setup: mock repository returns value; assertion: translator echoes value; catches mapping logic only, misses real DB access defects. Rejected System: no external OS/network/subprocess behavior beyond local DB integration.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temporary SQLite DB with known phrase mappings, invoke fallback translation for existing phrases, and assert exact match to stored translations.

behaviors.description: The quality tracking comparison correctly computes edit distance between primary and fallback outputs and stores the metric.
behaviors.justification: Although edit-distance math alone is Unit-testable, stated behavior also requires persistence of computed metric (compute + storage handoff). Candidate tests: Integration setup: run comparison pipeline with known string pairs and real metric store; assertion: persisted distance equals expected value; catches serialization/field-mapping/transaction bugs. Adjacent Unit setup: test pure editDistance(a,b); assertion numeric result; catches algorithm bugs but misses storage-path failures. Rejected Agentic: correctness here is deterministic string math, not model-quality judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Feed known primary/fallback string pairs with expected character distances through the tracking path and verify the exact metric value is written to storage.

behaviors.description: When both translators are available, the service returns the primary translation and logs the quality comparison in the background.
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration/state timing across components: primary result precedence + asynchronous background metric logging. Candidate tests: Integration setup: wire service with deterministic primary+fallback providers and real async job executor/store; assertion: response equals primary output and metric record appears eventually; catches race/order bugs. Adjacent System setup: include real HTTP/API infrastructure; assertion same; useful for transport realism but not required by behavior text. Rejected Agentic: behavior checks precedence and background side effect, not semantic quality of model output.
behaviors.test_description: With both translators enabled and deterministic outputs, assert synchronous response uses primary translation, then assert an async quality-comparison record is persisted within a timeout.
